title: cheilanthes pringlei var. moncloviensis
intro: Cheilanthes pringlei var. moncloviensis, commonly known as Pringle's lip fern, is a rare and endemic species of fern found in the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae and is known for its unique lip-like structure on the underside of its fronds. Cheilanthes pringlei var. moncloviensis has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant's bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, may contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant and antiseptic effects.

side_effects: Cheilanthes pringlei var. moncloviensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may lead to liver damage, kidney problems, and an increased risk of bleeding due to its potential interactions with blood-thinning medications. In rare cases, improper use of Cheilanthes pringlei var. moncloviensis may cause cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat and decreased blood pressure.
